name: Crypto Institutional Adoption
description: Bitcoin ETF flows, stablecoin regulation, corporate BTC treasury moves, or major institutional crypto adoption

themes.name: Coinbase Payment License · Stablecoin Infrastructure
Trump Crypto Policy Volatility · BTC Price Swings

name: Defense Buildup & Military Contracts
description: Increased defense spending, major weapons contracts, or NATO frontier buildup

themes.name: Iran War Munitions Replenishment · Patriot Missile
Japan Defense Export Liberalization · Production Ramp
Combat Rescue Helicopter Upgrade · IR Countermeasures

name: Middle East Conflict · Oil War Premium
description:
When military conflicts or escalating sanctions occur in the 
  Middle East region (involving Iran, Israel, Red Sea/Strait of Hormuz), crude oil supply faces threats, causing oil prices to surge with a war premium and
   energy stocks to outperform.

themes.name: Iran Crisis Escalation · Oil War Premium

name: Agricultural Supply Shock
description: Disruptions to fertilizer, grain, or crop supply that drive commodity prices and benefit agri-commodity producers

themes.name: Fertilizer Supply Chain Disruption · Global Repricing

name: U.S.-China Tariff Escalation · Domestic Substitution
description:
Rising U.S. tariffs on Chinese semiconductors, 
  critical technologies, and strategic materials—combined with expanded export controls—trigger domestic substitution demand and upward revaluation of     
  upstream critical material suppliers in China.

themes.name: Allied Chip Export Controls · China Localization

name: Energy Transition Acceleration · AI Power Demand / Nuclear Renaissance
description:
Explosive power demand from 
  AI data centers, nuclear technology breakthroughs (SMR commercialization), and large-scale energy storage deployment are triggering a revaluation of     
  clean energy infrastructure, grid modernization assets, and nuclear supply chains.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power · Semiconductor Efficiency

name: GLP-1 / Obesity Drug Breakthrough
description: New clinical trial results, FDA approvals, or expanded indications for GLP-1 and obesity drugs

themes.name: Lilly Oral GLP-1 Launch · Obesity Drug Competition

name: Rare Earth Security · Mining Opportunities
description:
Western nations implementing supply security policies for
   critical strategic minerals (rare earths, gallium, germanium), or China restricting exports, triggers revaluation of U.S. rare earth mining companies.  
  This catalyzes investment opportunities in domestic rare earth production as countries seek to reduce dependence on Chinese supply chains.

themes.name: US Rare Earth Onshoring · Magnet Manufacturing

name: Hyperscaler Mega Capex · Photonics/Power Infrastructure
description:
Hyperscale cloud providers or neoclouds   
  announce gigawatt-scale AI datacenter commitments (≥$5B), triggering revaluation of upstream supply chain including optical modules, lasers, substrates, 
  and power equipment.

themes.name: Anthropic-Amazon $100B AI Deal · Compute Infrastructure

name: EV Supply Chain Shift
description: Shifts in EV battery supply chain, lithium dynamics, or tariff/policy changes affecting EV producers

themes.name: Chinese EV Price Competition · UK Market Disruption
EV Battery Arms Race · Ultra-Fast Charging
Ford-China EV Partnership · US Market Entry
GM EV Retreat · Gas Truck Pivot

name: NVDA/AVGO Strategic Investments · Capacity Lock Signals
description:
Major semiconductor companies like NVIDIA 
  and Broadcom make strategic equity investments in upstream suppliers or sign multi-year supply agreements, with capacity-locking signals triggering      
  repricing across comparable companies in the same sector.

themes.name: NVDA-Tower Deal · Specialty Foundry Capacity Lock
NVDA $4B Photonics Lock · Optical Interconnect Capacity

name: CPO & Photonics Rotation · Next-Gen Interconnect
description:
Accelerating commercialization of Co-Packaged      
  Optics and silicon photonics technologies is driving explosive demand for 400G/800G/1.6T optical interconnects, triggering a large-scale revaluation of 
  the photonics supply chain.

themes.name: AI Data Center Optical Interconnect · 5-Year Transition

name: Taiwan Strait Tensions · Semiconductor Supply Chain Repricing
description:
Escalating military tensions in the   
  Taiwan Strait create significant disruption risks to the global semiconductor supply chain, triggering a repricing of alternative fabrication capacity 
  such as INTC and GFS as geopolitical hedges.

themes.name: Taiwan Info War Escalation · Semiconductor Risk

name: Critical Component Shortages · Upstream Pricing Power
description:
Industry-wide shortages of critical components
   (chips, optical modules, capacitors, substrates, etc.) trigger a repricing of upstream suppliers' bargaining power. Supply constraints shift 
  negotiating leverage to component manufacturers, enabling margin expansion and valuation re-rating.

themes.name: DRAM Allocation Shift · Auto Chip Shortage

name: Consumer Spending Polarization
description: K-shaped consumer divergence: luxury demand strength coexisting with discount retail growth

themes.name: Low-Income Consumer Stress · Discount Retail Slowdown

name: Cell Therapy Manufacturing Automation
description: FDA April 2026 guidance enabling automated closed-loop CAR-T and cell therapy manufacturing, reducing production time from 4 weeks to 48 hours and cost from $450K to sub-$100K per dose. Industrialization wave unlocking solid tumor and autoimmune disease addressable markets previously economics-limited.

themes.name: Cell Therapy Manufacturing Automation · iPS Cost Breakthrough

name: Water Infrastructure Crisis
description: Cascading failures in aging US municipal water systems—from lead pipe contamination to treatment plant breakdowns—are forcing emergency federal/state spending and creating urgent demand for water treatment technology, infrastructure replacement, and monitoring systems. PFAS contamination mandates (EPA limit effective 2026) and drought-driven scarcity in Southwest are compounding capital requirements. This represents a $600B+ infrastructure replacement cycle over 10 years.

name: AI Inference Chip Arms Race
description: Hyperscalers and edge device makers are rapidly developing custom inference ASICs to reduce dependency on NVIDIA GPUs for deployment workloads. This shift is driven by cost-per-inference economics, power efficiency requirements, and the maturation of models moving from training to production at scale. The theme captures companies enabling custom silicon design (EDA, foundry services, IP licensing) and those pivoting from pure training to inference-optimized architectures.

name: Fed Dovish Pivot · Small-Cap Biotech / REITs
description:
The Federal Reserve has clearly shifted to a           
  rate-cutting cycle, creating a repricing opportunity for small-cap stocks, biotechnology companies, and REITs that benefit from lower interest rates.

name: Inflation Surprise · Inflation-Resistant Assets
description:
When CPI/PCE data significantly exceeds expectations
   or inflation expectations rise again, inflation-resistant assets such as gold and energy benefit from increased hedging demand and pricing power.

name: Paradigm AI Model Breakthrough · Compute Demand Surge
description:
Paradigm-shifting AI model releases           
  (architectural revolution or capability phase-transition) trigger market expectations of exponential compute demand growth, catalyzing revaluation of    
  upstream chip manufacturers and optical interconnect providers. Limited to true paradigm shifts only, excluding incremental model iterations.

name: Regulatory Green Light · FDA/SEC/FTC Major Approvals
description:
Captures breakthrough regulatory approvals 
  including FDA drug/device clearances, SEC financial product authorizations (ETFs/crypto), and FTC merger clearances that unlock growth potential and     
  trigger sector revaluations. These catalysts signal reduced regulatory overhang and open addressable markets for affected companies.

name: Port/Shipping Disruption · Freight Rates + Repositioning
description:
Major port closures, blockages, or attacks 
  on key shipping routes trigger freight rate spikes and revaluation of shipping equities. Disruptions create supply chain bottlenecks that drive pricing  
  power for carriers and infrastructure plays.

name: Fab Disruption/Outage · Alternative Capacity Revaluation
description:
Production disruptions at critical 
  semiconductor fabrication facilities due to earthquakes, fires, accidents, or natural disasters trigger revaluation of alternative capacity suppliers and
   memory markets. Supply constraints drive pricing power and market share shifts to unaffected manufacturers.

name: Macro Risk Regime Shift · VIX Spike / Flight to Safety
description:
Systematic macro shocks (recession         
  expectations, credit crises, geopolitical escalation) trigger sharp VIX spikes, driving large-scale capital rotation out of risk assets into safe havens 
  such as gold, Treasuries, and defensive consumer staples. This is a defensive/risk-off investment theme.

name: Reshoring & Industrial Policy · CHIPS Act / IRA
description:
U.S. industrial policy initiatives—including the 
  CHIPS Act, IRA clean energy subsidies, and friend-shoring mandates—are driving a capital expenditure wave across semiconductor manufacturing, clean      
  energy production, and defense supply chain repatriation. These policies incentivize domestic and allied-nation production, creating investment     
  opportunities in facilities, equipment, and infrastructure buildout.

name: Turnaround Recovery · Multi-Quarter Earnings Inflection
description:
Companies emerging from prolonged losses or 
  EBITDA turning positive, guidance revisions upward), triggering valuation re-rating from distressed discount to normalized multiples.

name: Computational Drug Design Platform Shift
description: First wave of AI-discovered drug candidates reaching Phase 2/3 with clinical validation, triggering major pharma M&A premiums for computational platforms. Multiple $1B+ acquisitions of AI-native biotechs (Recursion-Roche, Exscientia programs) proving model economics and accelerating platform proliferation. Differentiation emerging between foundation model generalists vs. vertical specialists.

name: GCC Sovereign Wealth Diversification
description: Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Qatar accelerating $200B+ technology infrastructure investments announced April 2026, targeting AI data centers, semiconductor fabs, and cloud infrastructure as oil-revenue diversification strategy. Direct equity stakes and supply agreements creating multi-year revenue visibility for select Western tech firms.

name: Quantum Computing Commercialization
description: Multiple quantum computing firms announcing production-scale systems and commercial customer pilots in April 2026, driven by enterprise demand for cryptography migration ahead of 2030 NIST quantum-safe mandates. Hardware error correction breakthroughs enabling 1000+ logical qubit systems creating investable infrastructure layer distinct from AI model training.

name: Tungsten Supply Crisis
description: China's April 2026 export restrictions on tungsten (85% global supply) targeting Western defense and EV industries, with tungsten carbide critical for armor-piercing munitions and EV battery thermal management. Prices surged 340% since April 14, triggering emergency stockpiling and North American mine restart announcements.

name: Space Infrastructure Commercialization
description: Rapid increase in commercial and defense satellite deployment driven by SpaceX Starship operational readiness, Amazon Kuiper production ramp, and Pentagon's Proliferated Low Earth Orbit (pLEO) architecture contracts. Launch costs declining 40-60% YoY enabling new economics for satellite constellations, space-based AI inference, and persistent ISR capabilities.

name: Carbon Dioxide Removal Procurement
description: Frontier Carbon procurement coalition (Stripe, Alphabet, Meta, Shopify, McKinsey) scaling from $1B to $6B+ commitments, pulling forward commercialization of Direct Air Capture (DAC), enhanced weathering, and biochar projects. 45Q tax credit expansion to $180/ton and California LCFS credit pricing >$200/ton creating viable unit economics for first-generation CDR facilities.

name: China Stimulus · Commodity / Industrial Metals Demand
description:
PBoC announces significant RRR cuts and rate  
  reductions, or Ministry of Finance unveils above-expected infrastructure/consumption stimulus, triggering upward revision in demand expectations for 
  commodities (copper, iron ore, oil). Mining, energy, and industrial stocks benefiting from China demand undergo revaluation.

name: Next-Gen Alzheimer's Therapeutics
description: Multiple Phase 2/3 trials are showing promising results for Alzheimer's treatments targeting tau protein, neuroinflammation, and synaptic restoration—moving beyond the amyloid-beta hypothesis that dominated for decades. Recent biomarker validation and earlier-stage patient enrollment strategies are accelerating clinical timelines. This represents a fundamental shift in Alzheimer's R&D with 8-10 novel mechanisms entering late-stage trials in 2026.

name: Medical Radioisotope Supply Chain
description: Critical shortage resolution and new production capacity for Actinium-225, Lutetium-177, and Radium-223 enabling commercialization wave of targeted alpha/beta therapies for oncology. Domestic production via cyclotrons and small modular reactors reducing dependency on European/Russian supply chains while new FDA approvals create pull-through demand.

name: USD Strength · Overseas Revenue Pressure
description:
The U.S. Dollar Index is strengthening significantly,      
  creating FX headwinds for technology and industrial companies with high overseas revenue exposure. This theme serves as a risk monitoring framework for 
  currency translation impacts on multinational earnings.

name: Space-Based Solar Power
description: Multiple government and private initiatives are advancing space-based solar power (SBSP) systems that collect solar energy in orbit and beam it to Earth via microwave transmission. Recent breakthroughs in lightweight photovoltaic panels, wireless power transmission efficiency (>40% demonstrated), and launch cost reductions below $1,000/kg are making previously theoretical concepts commercially viable. This represents a frontier energy solution targeting AI data center baseload power and remote/military applications.

name: Grid-Scale Energy Storage Deployment
description: Utility-scale battery storage deployments accelerating to 40+ GWh annually (3x 2024 levels) driven by IRA 45X Advanced Manufacturing Production Credits ($35/kWh), interconnection queue clearing, and renewable curtailment reaching economically painful levels in CAISO/ERCOT. Sodium-ion and iron-air long-duration technologies achieving commercial deployment beyond lithium-ion 4-hour systems.
